categories.face-search: Upload a photo, find matches across indexed web (PimEyes-style)
categories.reverse-image: Reverse image search with face-recognition features (Yandex, Google Lens)
categories.face-database: Face databases not publicly searchable (Clearview AI — LE-only) but with opt-out rights

accessModels.free: Anyone can run a search without paying
accessModels.freemium: Free tier shows limited/blurred results; paid unlocks full
accessModels.restricted: Not publicly accessible (law enforcement / enterprise only)

pimeyes.pricing: Free tier: blurred thumbnails + domain only. PROtect monitoring: $29.99+/mo. Unlimited search: $14.99-$299.99/mo tiered.
pimeyes.knownFor: The most infamous face-search service. Billions of web pages indexed. Has been used for stalking, doxxing, adult-content identification.
pimeyes.notes: Opt-out form is FREE (one-time) but approval is discretionary and can take 30-60 days. PROtect is the paid continuous-monitoring subscription. Investigated by multiple EU DPAs for GDPR violations.

steps: Open pimeyes.com
Click the large 'Upload your photo' button on homepage
Either use webcam or upload a clear front-facing selfie (jpg/png, max 5MB)
Complete the reCAPTCHA challenge
Wait ~15-30 seconds for results to render
Free account shows: thumbnails blurred, domain names visible. Paid account shows: full URLs + unblurred thumbnails
scanWalkthrough.verification: Results page displays 'X matches found' with grid of thumbnails + source domain list.
scanWalkthrough.privacyNote: PimEyes keeps your uploaded photo for 48h by default — create an account and delete it immediately after your scan, or use the 'Private Search' toggle (paid).

steps: Open pimeyes.com/en/opt-out-form (direct link)
Read and accept the disclaimers about irreversibility
Upload 1-3 clear photos of yourself (front-facing, face visible, sunglasses off)
Enter the email you want confirmations sent to
For EU/UK users: optionally cite GDPR Article 17 (right to erasure) in the message field
Submit the form
optOutWalkthrough.verification: Email confirmation arrives within 24-72h. Full removal takes 7-30 days. May require additional photo verification if matches are ambiguous.
optOutWalkthrough.tierOverrides: PROtect subscribers ($29.99+/mo) get continuous monitoring — new matches auto-flagged for removal. Free one-time opt-out is sufficient for most people but doesn't cover future indexing.

facecheck-id.knownFor: Positioned as 'find who a person really is'. Growing competitor to PimEyes. Less comprehensive index but less restrictive on result details at free tier.
facecheck-id.notes: FaceCheck markets heavily to romance-scam detection / dating-app verification. Quality is lower than PimEyes but still sufficient for doxxing.

steps: Open facecheck.id
Click 'Upload a photo' or drag an image to the upload box
Complete captcha
Wait ~10-20 seconds for results
Free tier: see thumbnails + partial URLs. Premium: full URLs + confidence scores
scanWalkthrough.verification: Results page displays 'Score X/100' match confidence with thumbnail results.

steps: Open facecheck.id/optout
Upload 1-2 photos of yourself (front-facing recommended)
Enter email for confirmation
Provide government ID or other identity proof (may be requested)
Submit
optOutWalkthrough.verification: Confirmation email within 24-48h. Removal typically 7-14 days.

findclone.jurisdiction: global (heavy Russian/Slavic index via VK.com scraping)
findclone.knownFor: Russian service that scrapes VKontakte (Russian Facebook) + wider web. Best coverage for anyone with Slavic/Russian online presence.
findclone.notes: Pay-per-search model. No free tier. Has been used by journalists to identify Russian soldiers in Ukraine (BBC, Bellingcat investigations). Also a stalking tool in domestic abuse cases.

steps: Open findclone.ru (interface is in Russian — use browser auto-translate)
Register an account (requires email + phone)
Purchase credits ($5 per search minimum)
Upload a photo
View results: matches primarily from VK.com + other Russian social networks
scanWalkthrough.verification: Paid results show VK profile links, photo sources, similarity scores.
scanWalkthrough.privacyNote: Uses your phone number for account — use a burner if concerned. Service has Russian jurisdiction — consider if you're OK with that.

steps: Open findclone.ru/contact
Compose email to support requesting removal of biometric data
Include your VK profile link (primary data source) if applicable
Request deletion of your facial signature from their index
If in EU: cite GDPR Article 17
optOutWalkthrough.verification: Response varies. Russian data protection law (152-FZ) does permit removal requests, but enforcement on foreign services is weak. Consider also opting out of VK directly.

lenso.knownFor: Newer (2024) Polish face-search service. Markets itself as 'GDPR-compliant' alternative. Smaller index than PimEyes but growing.
lenso.notes: EU jurisdiction means your GDPR rights are effectively enforceable. Less comprehensive index = lower privacy risk but also less useful as a scanning tool.

steps: Open lenso.ai
Click 'Upload Image' (accepts jpg/png/webp)
Choose 'Face search' mode (vs. general image search)
Wait ~10 seconds
Free tier shows a few results; create account for more
Open lenso.ai/contact
Request deletion under GDPR Article 17 (right to erasure)
Provide 1-2 photos of yourself for identity verification
Provide email for confirmation
optOutWalkthrough.verification: EU-based service — must respond within 30 days under GDPR. Removal typically 7-14 days.

tineye.knownFor: Oldest reverse-image search. Indexes 67B+ images. NOT face-specific — finds identical/near-duplicate images across the web.
tineye.notes: Unlike PimEyes, TinEye finds the same EXACT image (not other photos of same face). Good for tracking if your profile picture has been scraped/reused. Zero face-recognition component.

steps: Open tineye.com
Click 'Upload' or paste an image URL
TinEye returns all web pages that host the same or near-duplicate image
Results sorted by: best match, most changed, oldest, newest
No login required
scanWalkthrough.verification: Results page shows 'X results' with thumbnail grid + source page URLs.

steps: Open tineye.com/contact
TinEye doesn't index face data so there's nothing to 'opt out' per se
If a specific image of yours is indexed: request removal via their contact form
Better long-term fix: find each source page that hosts the image and contact THEIR webmaster
optOutWalkthrough.verification: TinEye responds to DMCA takedowns for copyright-owned images but doesn't have a blanket 'remove me' flow.

yandex-images.knownFor: Russian search engine with the BEST general-purpose reverse image search — including implicit face recognition. Widely used by OSINT researchers for identifying people in photos.
yandex-images.notes: Not officially a face-search tool, but its reverse-image algorithm is strong enough to find 'same face, different photo' matches. Routinely used in OSINT investigations (Bellingcat etc.). Russian jurisdiction is a data sovereignty concern.

steps: Open yandex.com/images
Click the camera icon in the search bar
Upload a photo or paste URL
Yandex returns exact + near-matches + 'similar faces' section
No login needed
scanWalkthrough.verification: Results include a 'Faces' or 'Similar' section below the exact-match results — this is where face-matching lives.

steps: Open yandex.com/support/search/troubleshooting/delete-snippet.html
Submit a request to remove specific URLs from search results
This removes Yandex's indexing but NOT the source website
For facial signature removal: harder — contact support@yandex-team.ru with identity proof
optOutWalkthrough.verification: Removal from search results typically 7-14 days if justified (copyright, dead link, personal info).
optOutWalkthrough.tierOverrides: Yandex will NOT delete its underlying face-recognition model based on your request. Only specific indexed pages can be removed.

google-lens.knownFor: Google's reverse image search. Deliberately limited face-matching (Google disabled direct face search after 2011 controversy). Still useful for finding duplicate images of you.
google-lens.notes: Google intentionally does NOT show 'same face, different photo' results. Will show: the same image on other sites, visually similar objects, products. Limited as a face-scanner but good for image-of-you tracking.

steps: Open lens.google.com (or right-click any image in Chrome → 'Search image with Google Lens')
Upload or paste image
Scroll results — 'visual matches' and 'exact matches' are the useful sections
Google does NOT surface face-match results
scanWalkthrough.verification: Lens shows 'visual matches' grid. If your face appears in a source that's been scraped into Common Crawl / Google index, it'll show up.

optOutWalkthrough.targetSetting: Remove personal info from Google Search
steps: Open support.google.com/websearch/troubleshooter/3111061 (direct link)
Use the 'Results about you' tool: myactivity.google.com/results-about-you
Enter your name, aliases, phone, email, address
Google flags matching results across its index including images
Review + approve removal requests (you get notified when new matches appear)
optOutWalkthrough.verification: Google's 'Results about you' tool emails you when new matches are found. Removal from Google search doesn't remove from source sites, but de-indexes them effectively.
optOutWalkthrough.tierOverrides: This covers Google Search + Google Images + Lens matches. Separate from YouTube/Maps/other Google surfaces.

clearview-ai.pricing: Law enforcement and private-sector clients only. Price per-seat, enterprise contracts.
clearview-ai.knownFor: The most controversial face database. 40+ billion images scraped from social media. Sued + fined in EU, UK, Canada, Australia. Used by 3000+ US police departments. Individual users CANNOT search it.
clearview-ai.notes: You can't scan yourself in Clearview — it's LE-only. But you CAN request removal under CCPA (California), GDPR (EU), or similar rights. Clearview is LEGALLY REQUIRED to delete your data upon request in many jurisdictions.

steps: Open clearview.ai/privacy/requests
Select your jurisdiction (CA = CCPA, EU/UK = GDPR, etc.)
Upload 1-2 clear photos of yourself (front-facing)
Provide government ID for identity verification (passport or driver's license)
State: 'I request deletion of my biometric data and all associated images under [CCPA §1798.105 / GDPR Article 17]'
Submit
optOutWalkthrough.verification: Required to respond within 45 days (CCPA) or 30 days (GDPR). Clearview has complied with thousands of requests. Deletion is (reportedly) honored — but verification is difficult since you can't search their index yourself.
optOutWalkthrough.tierOverrides: Under Illinois BIPA, Clearview is banned from collecting biometric data of IL residents — if you're in Illinois, you have stronger rights. Class action settlements have required blanket deletions (Illinois 2022).
